raw:
{
  "recommended_pattern": "Contextual Executive Overlay Menu",
  "why": [
    "Provides discreet, always-available access to executive actions from the operational view.",
    "Minimizes context switching by initiating executive tasks without a full page redirect or 'mode' change.",
    "Leverages existing /ceo/* routes, reducing implementation complexity and risk for a V1.1."
  ],
  "ui_shape": [
    "A small, persistent icon (e.g., a crown or gear) visible only to the CEO, ideally in a fixed corner (e.g., bottom-right).",
    "Clicking the icon opens a compact, non-blocking overlay menu on top of the current /auditar view.",
    "The overlay menu lists clear, descriptive links to the existing /ceo/* executive console routes."
  ],
  "routing_strategy": [
    "The CEO remains on /auditar as the primary base view.",
    "Clicking a link within the executive overlay menu will perform a standard browser navigation to the corresponding /ceo/* route.",
    "The overlay itself is dismissible, allowing the CEO to return to the full /auditar view without navigation."
  ],
  "security_guardrails": [
    "The executive UI element and overlay must be rendered only if the authenticated user has the 'CEO' role (backend check).",
    "All /ceo/* routes must retain their existing, robust backend authorization checks, preventing access by non-CEO users.",
    "The 'admin as user' simulation mode must explicitly *not* display this executive overlay, ensuring normal users (even simulated) do not see CEO actions."
  ],
  "implementation_order": [
    "1. Backend: Implement a simple API endpoint or extend current user context to expose the 'isCEO' role flag.",
    "2. Frontend (UI Element): Create the persistent icon component, conditionally rendered based on 'isCEO' flag.",
    "3. Frontend (Overlay Menu): Develop the overlay component, populating it with links to existing /ceo/* routes.",
    "4. Frontend (Integration): Integrate the icon and overlay into the main /auditar layout, ensuring it doesn't interfere with existing elements.",
    "5. Frontend (Simulation Mode): Update the 'admin as user' logic to explicitly hide this new executive UI element."
  ],
  "avoid": [
    "Embedding entire /ceo/* pages or complex executive dashboards directly within the /auditar view (high complexity, potential security risks).",
    "Degrading or bypassing existing backend authorization for /ceo/* routes.",
    "Making the executive UI element visible to any user without the explicit 'CEO' role, even if the routes are protected.",
    "Over-engineering the overlay with interactive executive actions that require significant frontend state management or API calls beyond simple navigation."
  ]
}

why: Provides discreet, always-available access to executive actions from the operational view.
Minimizes context switching by initiating executive tasks without a full page redirect or 'mode' change.
Leverages existing /ceo/* routes, reducing implementation complexity and risk for a V1.1.
ui_shape: A small, persistent icon (e.g., a crown or gear) visible only to the CEO, ideally in a fixed corner (e.g., bottom-right).
Clicking the icon opens a compact, non-blocking overlay menu on top of the current /auditar view.
The overlay menu lists clear, descriptive links to the existing /ceo/* executive console routes.
routing_strategy: The CEO remains on /auditar as the primary base view.
Clicking a link within the executive overlay menu will perform a standard browser navigation to the corresponding /ceo/* route.
The overlay itself is dismissible, allowing the CEO to return to the full /auditar view without navigation.
security_guardrails: The executive UI element and overlay must be rendered only if the authenticated user has the 'CEO' role (backend check).
All /ceo/* routes must retain their existing, robust backend authorization checks, preventing access by non-CEO users.
The 'admin as user' simulation mode must explicitly *not* display this executive overlay, ensuring normal users (even simulated) do not see CEO actions.
implementation_order: 1. Backend: Implement a simple API endpoint or extend current user context to expose the 'isCEO' role flag.
2. Frontend (UI Element): Create the persistent icon component, conditionally rendered based on 'isCEO' flag.
3. Frontend (Overlay Menu): Develop the overlay component, populating it with links to existing /ceo/* routes.
4. Frontend (Integration): Integrate the icon and overlay into the main /auditar layout, ensuring it doesn't interfere with existing elements.
5. Frontend (Simulation Mode): Update the 'admin as user' logic to explicitly hide this new executive UI element.
avoid: Embedding entire /ceo/* pages or complex executive dashboards directly within the /auditar view (high complexity, potential security risks).
Degrading or bypassing existing backend authorization for /ceo/* routes.
Making the executive UI element visible to any user without the explicit 'CEO' role, even if the routes are protected.
Over-engineering the overlay with interactive executive actions that require significant frontend state management or API calls beyond simple navigation.
